rulings.Case Title: VINCENT WALTENBERGER, ET VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 12, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. THE MOTION IS DENIED. IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION, BASED ON THE ANALYSIS IN BAEZ V. BOYLE & CO. (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 700, 717, THAT JURISDICTION IS DETERMINED AT THE TIME OF EXPOSURE, NOT WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED. AT THAT TIME, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS TO ASSERT GENERAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. AS TO SPECIAL JURISDICTION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE OCCURRED IN WISCONSIN AND THAT THERE IS NOT A BASIS TO ASSERT SPECIAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. SEE CASSIER MINING CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 550, 555. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL ELAURIA et al VS. SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer is overruled with 10 days to answer. The Court may take judicial notice of the arbitration award between SSSF and plaintiff. Kilroy v. State (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 140, 148; Flores v. Arroyo (1961) 56 Cal.2d 492, 496. The issue is the effect of the award. SSI and LaVoy were not parties to the arbitration agreement entered into between plaintiff and SSSF. As a consequence, the collateral estoppel effect of the arbtration decision is governed by Vandenberg v. Superior Court (1999) 21 Cal.4th 815, 834: "Accordingly, we are compelled to conclude that a private arbitration award, even if judicially confirmed, can have no collateral estoppel effect in favor of third persons unless the arbitral parties agreed, in the particular case, that such a consequence should apply." At the same time, Vandenberg stressed that the decision is limited and that the Court did not intend to overturn several cases, including Lehto v. Underground Construction Co. (1977) 69 Cal.App.3d 933, 939, addressing the res judicata effect of an arbitration award. Plaintiffs' agree that that their claims would be barred by res judicata - claim preclusion - if the present claims are derivative and identical to plaintiffs' claims against SSSF, citing Bernhard v. Bank of America (1942) 19 Cal.2d 807, 812-813. (Pls. Further Briefing in Opposition to Demurrer, page 6.) Plaintiffs have alleged facts related to LaVoy and SSI that the Court must accept as true for the purpose of the demurrer that assert a relationship with these defendants that is not derived from the relationship with SSSF. See for example paragraphs 24-26. Although these facts appear to be inconsistent with the terms of the SSSF franchise agreement, the Court cannot conclude, as a matter of law, that pltfs are unable to prove a separate and distinct relationship with these defts. The demurrer is based solely on the res judicata effect of the arbitration award and that is the only issue the Court is addressing. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUELO LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 12, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT PFIZER INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. AT THE SAME TIME, IT IS APPARENT THAT PLAINTIFFS' LACK EVIDENCE TO PROVE EXPOSURE AND THAT DEFENDANT HAS RAISED SERIOUS QUESTIONS AS TO PLAINTIFFS' GOOD FAITH COMPLIANCE WITH THE DISCOVERY MANDATES OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE WITH RESPECT TO THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS AND DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION NOTICES. AT THE SAME TIME, DEFENDANT WAS SERVED ON DECEMBER 31, 2007 BUT DID NOT SERVE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES UNTIL JULY 23, 2008. DEFENDANTS ALSO HAVE NOT SOUGHT A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE BASED ON PLAINTIFFS' DISCOVERY CONDUCT. THE COURT ADMITS THE TESTIMONY OF GENCO AND METTRIC BASED ON THE REPLY EVIDENCE BUT THAT TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHILDREN'S DAY SCHOOL VS. ROBERT J MCCARTHY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 12, 2008, line 19. DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE PROBATE CODE DOES NOT NECESSARILY REQUIRE WRITTEN ACTUAL NOTICE TO THE CREDITOR IS THE ONLY FORM OF NOTICE THAT MAY BE GIVEN. THE PURPOSE OF NOTICE IS TO PROTECT THE PARTIES. WHERE THE TRUSTEE AND CREDITOR AGREE TO ORAL NOTICE, THERE IS NO REASON WHY THAT IS INSUFFICIENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE TRUSTEE'S ATTORNEY PROVIDED ORAL NOTICE ON 3/17/03 AND THAT PLAINTIFF, IN TURN, FILED A TIMELY CLAIM ON 4/11/03. =(302/PJM/PB)
